intro: Account currency and reporting currency answer different questions in enterprise finance. Account currency governs how balances are maintained and controlled inside accounts; reporting currency governs how measured results are presented to management, investors, regulators, or group reporting audiences.
doctrine_statement:
Account currency is custody. Reporting currency is presentation.
Confusing them turns visibility into false control.
primary_section_title: One controls balances; the other presents results.
primary_section_body: Account currency sits at State 04 of the CCY State Chain. It attaches currency identity to accounts, balances, and reconciliation scopes.
Reporting currency sits at State 06. It translates measured ledger results into a presentation view for reporting, governance, or disclosure.
The two may share the same ISO code in simple structures, but they remain different custody states with different evidence requirements.

steps.title: Account currency
steps.body: Account-level custody setting that controls balance maintenance, reconciliation scope, and downstream revaluation behavior.

steps.title: Reporting currency
steps.body: Presentation currency used to tell the financial story after ledger measurement stabilizes.

steps.title: Revaluation and translation
steps.body: The mechanics that often expose confusion between custody and presentation.

pillar_sections.title: Different evidence proves each currency identity
paragraphs: Account currency evidence includes account master data, policy matrices, configuration approvals, reconciliation scope, and change logs.
Reporting currency evidence includes translation methodology, reporting packages, bridge schedules, management commentary support, and disclosure reconciliations.
When teams use one evidence set to defend the other, audit reality weakens.

pillar_sections.title: Control questions for the distinction
questions: Which account currency governs maintained balances?
Which reporting currency governs external or management presentation?
Where does ledger measurement occur between the two?
Which evidence proves account custody versus reporting translation?
Are FX effects being explained as operational, remeasurement, or presentation effects?
Can reviewers trace reporting numbers back to account-level custody without informal bridges?

graph_title: Account currency is not reporting currency.
graph_intro: This comparison protects a high-value SEO intent while reinforcing AccountCcy's state architecture.

cards.label: Custody
cards.title: Account currency controls maintained balances.
cards.body: It belongs to account configuration, reconciliation scope, and revaluation populations.

cards.label: Presentation
cards.title: Reporting currency controls visibility.
cards.body: It belongs to management reporting, disclosures, translation, and external communication.

cards.label: Evidence
cards.title: Each requires different proof.
cards.body: Configuration extracts cannot replace reporting bridges, and reporting bridges cannot prove account custody.

control_body: Account-level configuration and reporting translation should reconcile, but they should not collapse into one control object.
risk_body: Teams may explain reported figures while losing the ability to prove how the underlying balances were maintained.

faq.question: Can account currency and reporting currency be the same code?
faq.answer: Yes, but they still answer different control questions and require different evidence.

faq.question: Which comes first?
faq.answer: Account currency custody precedes reporting currency presentation in the CCY State Chain.
